headline: INNOVATING THE FRAME: THE AI PRODUCTION REVOLUTION
subtitle: AI Video Production News

stories.title: The Newsroom Stops Thinking in Rundowns
stories.summary: Cuez is using NAB 2026 to pitch a newsroom model built around stories rather than bulletins. Its new Storydesk, automation blocks and open AI agent framework let teams repurpose editorial assets across TV, digital and social without copy-paste chaos.

stories.title: Dalet's Bet on Agentic Media Ops
stories.summary: Dalet's new Dalia layer wraps its media stack in a natural-language interface, while Flex and Pyramid push semantic search, browser-based collaboration and Adobe-friendly workflows. It is a clear sign that AI is becoming the control surface for enterprise media operations.

stories.title: Broadcast-Quality Town Halls, No Full Crew Required
stories.summary: QuickLink's StudioPro Town Hall aims to give lightly staffed organisations broadcast-grade live coverage by automating switching, active-speaker tracking and graphics. That makes AI feel less like a toy and more like a genuine crew multiplier for civic, corporate and education events.

stories.title: Panasonic Pushes AI Into Live Sports and Podcast Workflows
stories.summary: Panasonic's NAB setup leans into the reality that live streaming, podcasting and venue production now need to look premium on leaner crews. Its pitch combines AI-assisted PTZ and IP workflows with LED-heavy sports and event production.
stories.alt: AI-driven PTZ sports and podcast production

stories.title: Voice-Controlled Live Production Is Here
stories.summary: Action Phrase takes a delightfully direct approach to production automation: speak a command and trigger cameras, graphics, layouts or scripts hands-free. It is early, but it points to a future where live direction becomes faster, more accessible and less tied to traditional control surfaces.
